sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, encoded_data VARCHAR(255), search_col TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, encoded_data, search_col) VALUES (1, ':5''9XE''m:', 'sample keyword for testing');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, encoded_data, search_col) VALUES (2, ':5''9XE''m:', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, encoded_data, search_col) VALUES (3, ':5''9XE''m:', '');
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE bif:contains(search_col, uudecode(encoded_data, 0));
SELECT id, uudecode(encoded_data, 0) AS original_data FROM test_data WHERE bif:contains(search_col, 'sample');
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS total FROM test_data GROUP BY uudecode(encoded_data, 0) HAVING total > 1;
SELECT * FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON uudecode(t1.encoded_data, 0) = uudecode(t2.encoded_data, 0) WHERE t1.id != t2.id;
SELECT t.id, COALESCE(uudecode(t.encoded_data, 0), 'Decoded data not available') AS decoded_data FROM test_data t WHERE bif:contains(t.search_col, 'testing');
SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE search_col IS NULL) > 0 THEN 'NULL values exist' ELSE 'No NULL values' END AS null_check;
